title: Figlet Cheat Sheet
description: A quick reference for using Figlet to create ASCII art text in the terminal.

commands.scenario: Use when you want to create an ASCII art banner for 'Hello World'.
Use when you need a slanted style for your ASCII art text.

commands.description: Center the ASCII art text output.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to center the text output in your terminal.

commands.description: Set the output width to 100 columns.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to adjust the width of the ASCII art output to fit your terminal.

commands.description: Enable kerning for the ASCII art text.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to adjust the spacing between characters in the ASCII art.

commands.description: Generate ASCII art using the 'banner' font.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to create large ASCII art text with the 'banner' font.

commands.description: Left align the ASCII art text output.
commands.scenario: Use when you need the text to be left-aligned in your terminal.

commands.description: Right align the ASCII art text output.
commands.scenario: Use when you need the text to be right-aligned in your terminal.

commands.description: Fit the ASCII art to the terminal width.
commands.scenario: Use when you want the ASCII art to automatically fit the terminal width.

commands.description: Use the 'digital' font for ASCII art.
commands.scenario: Use when you want a digital-style font for your ASCII art text.

commands.description: Generate ASCII art using the 'small' font.
commands.scenario: Use when you need a compact ASCII art text with the 'small' font.

commands.description: Use the 'standard' font for ASCII art.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to create ASCII art text with the default 'standard' font.
